name: PyNWB (Python Neurodata Without Borders)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); NWB Team / Allen Institute, Salk Institute

performance.notes: HDF5-based; lazy loading for large datasets; remfile for streaming

agent_readiness.notes: NWB format for neurophysiology data sharing. import pynwb. io = pynwb.NWBHDF5IO('file.nwb', 'r'). nwbfile = io.read(). nwbfile.acquisition. nwbfile.units. nwbfile.electrodes. nwbfile.trials. Create: nwbfile = pynwb.NWBFile(session_description=, identifier=, session_start_time=). nwbfile.add_unit(spike_times=[...]). Write: io.write(nwbfile). Streaming: from remfile import File; io = pynwb.NWBHDF5IO(file=File(url)). DANDI Archive integration. Electrophysiology, behavior, imaging modalities.

gotchas: NWB schema is complex — requires neuroscience domain knowledge
HDF5 file locking issues on NFS/network filesystems
Large NWB files need lazy loading — accessing .data[:] loads everything
Writing NWB files requires careful schema compliance
best_when: Reading/writing NWB format neural data for DANDI Archive sharing
Standardized electrophysiology, behavior, and calcium imaging data
Streaming remote NWB files from DANDI without downloading
Multi-session neuroscience analysis with standardized format
avoid_when: When HDF5 with custom schema is simpler
Spike sorting pipelines (use SpikeInterface for preprocessing)
summary: PyNWB provides the Python API for Neurodata Without Borders (NWB) format. NWBHDF5IO('file.nwb', 'r').read(). nwbfile.units, .acquisition, .electrodes. Create/write NWB files. DANDI Archive streaming via remfile. BSD-3. Standard format for neurophysiology data sharing. Required for DANDI Archive. Complex schema — requires neuroscience knowledge. Stream remote files without downloading.
